user_message: I need you to process this exec meeting notes from Wide World Importers. The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-01-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Wide World Importers)

[00:02:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on pipeline hygiene from our last session.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:12:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:17:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:20:00] David (Wide World Importers): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:22:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:24:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Wide World Importers: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF51-B trap (Confuses partner aspirational role with committed role); produces complete Tri-party action distribution (MS/partner/customer owners), funding eligibility assessment, follow-u
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF51-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF51-B failure mode (Confuses partner aspirational role with committed role)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF51-B -- Confuses partner aspirational role with committed role
